differences.title: 1. INITIAL RENDER
differences.leftDesc: Initial HTML sent from server, reducing client load.
differences.rightDesc: Requires JavaScript to render UI in the browser.

differences.title: 2. DATA FETCHING
differences.leftDesc: Can fetch data directly on the server, faster initial loads.
differences.rightDesc: Relies on client-side API calls, potentially slower initial loads.

differences.title: 3. INTERACTIVITY
differences.leftDesc: Cannot use hooks like useState or onClick directly.
differences.rightDesc: Full interactivity with hooks and event listeners.

differences.title: 4. BUNDLE SIZE
differences.leftDesc: Doesn't send component JavaScript to the client.
differences.rightDesc: Sends JavaScript to the client for rendering and interactivity.

differences.title: 5. SEO
differences.leftDesc: Content is pre-rendered on the server, great for search engines.
differences.rightDesc: Relies on JavaScript execution for content, can impact SEO.

differences.title: 6. ACCESS TO RESOURCES
differences.leftDesc: Can directly access server resources and databases.
differences.rightDesc: Limited to browser-available APIs and DOM.

differences.title: 7. CODE EXECUTION
differences.leftDesc: Code runs exclusively on the server, no client-side JS.
differences.rightDesc: Code runs entirely in the user's browser.
